rows.notes: Clear centered page title at top of sheet; no monetary columns present on this line.
Clear sub-heading beneath the title; no amounts visible in the £/s/d columns.
Pounds (172), shillings (7) and pence (11) with a clear halfpenny mark '½' are well aligned with the vertical ruling; digits are distinct.
Pounds '255' clearly written in the pound column; the shillings and pence columns are blank (visible dashes/spaces), so treated as empty.
Single clear pounds figure '15' in the pound column; shillings and pence columns empty.
Pounds '65' and shillings '19' align well; pence shows '1' with a small halfpenny sign '½'. Slight uncertainty (0.9) because the place name spelling is cramped but amounts and fraction are clear.
Clear reading of 19 pounds, 7 shillings, 2 pence; all three columns legible and validate (s<20, d<12).
Clear '28' in pounds, '6' in shillings and '10' in pence; alignment with ruling lines is unambiguous.

Pounds '35' and shillings '1' clear. Pence read as '11' with a small '3/4' (three farthings) sign; interpreted as fraction 0.75. Slight uncertainty due to small fractional mark but alignment supports the reading.
Pounds '14', shillings '2' and pence '5' with a clear halfpenny '½'. The dash/ditto in description is legible; amounts align properly.
Clear 29 pounds, 6 shillings and 1 penny; fractional mark absent and digits are distinct.
Pounds '8', shillings '10' and pence '1' are clearly visible and well aligned to the right columns.
Values read as 12/19/11½; the halfpenny is clearly marked and columns align precisely.

Pounds '11' and shillings '2' are clear. In the pence column there is no obvious whole pence numeral but a very small '¼' (farthing) mark is visible. Interpreted as 0 pence and a farthing (0.25). Lower confidence due to absent whole-pence digit and the fractional mark being small and slightly faded.
Pounds '4', shillings '5' and pence '1' are visible; pence shows a clear '3/4' (three farthings) notation. Slight lowering from 1.0 only because the fractional mark is small but unambiguous.
Centered section heading introducing the following group; no monetary figures on this header line.
Pounds '22', shillings '5' and pence '2' with a halfpenny '½' are legible and aligned. Minor uncertainty due to slight ink blurring in the description only.
Pounds '10', shillings '18' and pence '9' with halfpenny '½' clearly visible. Ink around the shillings is slightly darker but numbers read unambiguously.
Clear '10' pounds, '5' shillings and '11½' pence; the halfpenny sign is visible next to the 11 pence.

Pounds '17' and shillings '1' are clearly written. The pence column shows a dash (no figure). Interpreted as no pence recorded; moderate confidence because the dash could indicate zero or an omitted entry.
Pounds '2' and shillings '18' are clear. Pence looks like '6' with a small '¼' (farthing) mark next to it. The fraction is somewhat faint and the '6' has minor ink bleed, so confidence reduced.
